title: Transformer Models for Low-Resource African Languages: A Case Study in Yoruba and Igbo
excerpt: Novel fine-tuning approach improving translation accuracy by 47% for Yoruba and Igbo.
author: Dr. Aisha Bello
authorTitle: Lead AI Researcher, University of Lagos
content:
Artificial Intelligence has made tremendous progress in natural language processing, yet low-resource languages such as Yoruba, Igbo, Hausa, and Swahili continue to lag behind.

Our Approach

We fine-tuned a multilingual transformer model using transfer learning and culturally relevant datasets. Our model achieved a BLEU score improvement of 47% compared to baseline models.

Key Findings

The inclusion of indigenous proverbs significantly improved contextual understanding.

title: Autonomous Agricultural Drones: Enhancing Food Security in Sub-Saharan Africa
excerpt: Low-cost drones reducing pesticide usage by 60% in Sub-Saharan Africa.
author: Engr. Olumide Adeyemi
content: Food insecurity remains a critical challenge across Sub-Saharan Africa. This paper presents the design and deployment of low-cost autonomous drones tailored for smallholder farmers.

title: AI-Driven Early Detection of Malaria Using Smartphone Microscopy
excerpt: 96.4% accuracy model for rural healthcare settings.
author: Dr. Fatima Nkosi
content: Malaria continues to claim hundreds of thousands of lives annually in Africa. This research introduces a lightweight deep learning model capable of detecting malaria parasites from smartphone-captured microscopic images with 96.4% accuracy.

title: Ethical Frameworks for Responsible AI Development in Africa
excerpt: Culturally grounded ethical framework tailored to African values.
author: Prof. Naledi Khumalo
content: This paper proposes a culturally grounded ethical framework for AI development in Africa.
